comment: Leadership-voice release notes. Hand-written, paired with the team-voice tagline pulled from the matching git commit subject. Add a new entry at the top of the array whenever a PATCH or larger release ships.

releases.leadership: Added a Portfolio heatmap, accessible from the new Heatmap link in the top navigation. The page shows a Goal × Department grid with each cell colored by project count, surfacing where data program work concentrates today. Filters apply to the heatmap the same way they apply elsewhere. The page includes a 'What's next' note explaining how this view will evolve to show actual time invested once tracker time-entry data is exposed.

The portfolio page now offers a By department view. Use the dropdown above the cards to switch between By goal (the original view, six Data Program Goals) and By department (one card per partner department, sorted by project count). Clicking a department card applies that department as a filter and switches back to the goal view, showing how that department's work distributes across the program.

Removed the What Works Cities tab. The wwc_practice and wwc_criteria tags applied by the data team reflect aspirational alignment rather than projects formally approved or certified by the WWC committee, so framing them as a 'certification' view risked misrepresenting the city's status. The underlying tags remain in the tracker for internal use; we may revisit a public view if and when WWC certification status itself becomes part of the dataset.

Added a What Works Cities certification view, accessible from the new WWC link in the top navigation. The page maps each Data Program project to the WWC certification framework's eight practice areas and 43 criteria, showing how Tucson's data work supports certification. Each criterion lists the projects tagged to it, clickable for full project details. Practice areas without any tagged projects in the current view are hidden so the page focuses on coverage that exists today.

Pages now flag when their data has gone stale. If you leave the site open in a tab for more than 15 minutes, a small bar appears under the top navigation showing how long the data has been loaded, with a Refresh button that pulls fresh data from the tracker without a full page reload. Filter state and your current page are preserved through the refresh.

Filter views are now shareable and persistent. A new Share button in the top navigation copies the current URL — including any active filters — to your clipboard so you can paste it into email, Slack, or a bookmark and the recipient sees the same filtered view. Filters also now carry across page navigation: filtering on Home and clicking Roadmap or Portfolio preserves the filter, instead of resetting it.

Added a Filter feature so leadership can narrow the portfolio by status, goal, and department. Filtered views are shareable — the URL captures the filter state, so a link sent over email or Slack opens the same filtered view for the recipient. Status strip and goal cards across every page reflect the active filter, including the new filter banner under the top navigation that lists what's filtered and lets you clear individual chips.
Added the City of Tucson Information Technology logo to the page header and footer, completing institutional attribution alongside the Tucson DATA mark.
Added a 'What's new' page so anyone can see what's changed release over release. The project detail modal now shows a content-shaped placeholder while it loads, in place of plain 'Loading…' text.
Phase 1 production launch. Tucson DATA goes live with home, roadmap, portfolio, and per-goal detail pages — a leadership-facing window onto the City of Tucson Data Team's program portfolio.
